data.requirement: Integrate crop production data with soil analysis data by aligning on the common 'District' column to analyze the relationship between soil type and crop yield.
data.reason: District-based alignment links soil properties to crop yield measures, essential for understanding local agricultural performance.

data.requirement: Aggregate nutrient content data (e.g., organic matter, nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium) at the district level to characterize typical soil profiles in each district.
data.reason: Aggregated metrics allow for district-level analysis of soil quality and facilitate direct comparison with yield outcomes.

data.requirement: Highlight top-performing districts for specific nutrients by finding extrema (max/min) values and their correlation with crop yield.
data.reason: Identifying high or low nutrient performance districts provides actionable insights for targeted soil improvement plans.

data.requirement: Enable filtering data by specific crops and soil types to examine nuanced relationships between soil properties and crop yield.
data.reason: Crop-specific evaluations are critical for linking soil suitability to the respective crops grown in each district.

data.requirement: Break down yield data into distribution summaries (e.g., mean, median, quartiles) for each combination of soil type and crop.
data.reason: Characterizing distributions allows for the identification of variability in yield performance under different soil conditions.

data.requirement: Introduce soil nutrient ratios (e.g., nitrogen-to-potassium) as derived metrics for analysis to provide deeper insights into soil quality.
data.reason: Derived ratios can reveal balance-related patterns that affect crop yields more significantly than individual nutrients alone.

data.requirement: Generate a macro-level overview comparing aggregate crop yields across districts based on dominant soil types.
data.reason: A high-level view helps to establish overarching trends and associations before diving into detailed analyses.

data.requirement: Enable comparison between neighboring districts' soil properties and yields for geographical correlation analysis.
data.reason: Proximity-based comparisons may reveal regional patterns related to shared environmental or agricultural conditions.

visualization.requirement: Use a heat map to show the correlation between nutrient content and crop yields across districts for each soil type.
visualization.reason: Heat maps effectively visualize multivariate correlations, making them ideal for revealing relationships between nutrients and yield.

visualization.requirement: Create bar charts for each district showing the average, maximum, and minimum yield for crops based on soil type and nutrient availability.
visualization.reason: Bar charts facilitate comparison both within and across districts for soil type-specific yield performance.

visualization.requirement: Include scatter plots for analyzing correlations between paired nutrient content values (e.g., nitrogen vs. potassium) and yield.
visualization.reason: Scatter plots help to visualize potential relationships or trade-offs between soil nutrient levels and crop yields.

visualization.requirement: Provide box plots of yield distributions for different soil types within each crop.
visualization.reason: Box plots are ideal for summarizing distributions, highlighting variability, and identifying outliers in crop yield data.

visualization.requirement: Use pie charts to show the proportion of soil types in each district and their associated yield performance.
visualization.reason: Pie charts give an intuitive representation of the overall contribution of soil types to district yield performance.

visualization.requirement: Include line charts to track yield trends over time for key combinations of soil types and nutrient availability.
visualization.reason: Line charts are helpful for time-series analysis, capturing how yields fluctuate across different soil profiles.

visualization.requirement: Create a tick chart comparing nutrient levels across districts for a selected crop or soil type.
visualization.reason: Tick charts can highlight variations and benchmarks for nutrients quickly and clearly.

visualization.requirement: Use a simple combined chart (e.g., bar and scatter on the same axes) to examine the relationship between area cultivated and yield across different districts.
visualization.reason: Combined charts can reveal the interplay between land use and productivity in specific districts.

interaction.requirement: Enable filtering by district, crop, and soil type through drop-down or checklist menus.
interaction.reason: This allows users to focus on specific geographic areas, crops, or soil conditions for a more targeted analysis.

interaction.requirement: Allow dynamic hover interactions to display detailed metadata (e.g., exact nutrient values, soil type, crop type, yield) in visualizations.
interaction.reason: Hover interactions provide contextual details while keeping the overall visualization uncluttered.

interaction.requirement: Enable brushing and linking across multiple visualizations to highlight related data points (e.g., select a district and see its values propagate in all views).
interaction.reason: This interaction ensures that users can see multivariate relationships and patterns across the entire analysis pipeline.

interaction.requirement: Add sliders or range selectors to adjust thresholds for nutrient levels or yield ranges and dynamically update visualizations.
interaction.reason: Range filtering is crucial for finding trends in desired nutrient or yield thresholds, making the system more informative.

interaction.requirement: Integrate selection of soil type in pie charts to dynamically filter other visualizations (e.g., bar charts or scatter plots).
interaction.reason: This interaction facilitates cross-view exploration and helps users see specific soil types' influence on yield performance.

interaction.requirement: Allow grouping and comparison of multiple districts in scatter or heat map visualizations through multi-select capabilities.
interaction.reason: This enables users to conduct regional analyses and compare performance across similar or neighboring districts.

interaction.requirement: Include drill-down functionality from macro-level overviews (e.g., yield trends across districts) to micro-level details (e.g., yield for specific crops).
interaction.reason: Drill-downs enable users to explore both broad trends and specific details in a seamless manner.

interaction.requirement: Incorporate interactive annotations to add user insights or comments to specific data points on visualizations.
interaction.reason: Annotations allow users to document observations or hypotheses, enhancing collaborative or personal research efforts.
